text.title: Subscription Model Types and Tier Design
text.summary: Five subscription model types with best-fit guidance; billing frequency trade-offs; Good-Better-Best, Freemium, and Usage+Base tier structures; feature allocation rules.

list.item: SaaS — software access. Users pay for ongoing access to a platform or tool. Best for: tools, platforms, productivity software.
Membership — community or content access. Users pay for belonging and exclusive content. Best for: education, professional communities, newsletters.
Replenishment — regular physical product delivery. Best for: consumables, personal care, food.
Curation — curated selections delivered periodically. Best for: discovery products (book clubs, music, fashion boxes).
Access — exclusive availability (not content, but priority or privilege). Best for: premium services, concierge products.

rule.statement: Offer both monthly and annual billing. Present annual as "2 months free" (not "17% off"). For most SaaS, annual reduces churn by 3–5x compared to monthly-only.
rule.rationale: Annual billing creates a 12-month commitment window before the next churn decision. "2 months free" is tangible (users can visualize two months); "17% off" is abstract.

rule.statement: Use Good-Better-Best (max 4 tiers) as default. Free tier must be limited enough that a real user would want to upgrade within 30 days of hitting the limit.
rule.rationale: Too-generous free tiers eliminate upgrade motivation. The most common subscription failure pattern is a free tier that fully satisfies the core use case.

example.note: Good-Better-Best for a SaaS tool — core value limited on free, full access on Pro, collaboration on Team.
example.code: Basic ($9/mo): Core features, 100 items limit Pro ($29/mo): Full features, unlimited (anchor tier) Team ($79/mo): Collaboration + priority support

example.note: Usage + Base for API-driven product — base covers fixed costs, usage covers variable.
example.code: Base ($29/mo): Platform access, 1K API calls included Usage: $0.01 per additional API call
